sourceRef: tip:Six questions to ask at every intake

slides.headline: Streamline Intake
slides.body: Automate your workflow and catch issues early.

slides.headline: Is the student a veteran?
slides.body: Ask for proof of service to ensure eligibility.
bullets: Check the student's ID for military service dates.

slides.headline: Verify the COE
slides.body: Confirm the student's Certificate of Eligibility.
bullets: Refer to Chapter 33 for the COE process.

slides.headline: Check Chapter 33 eligibility
slides.body: Ensure the student qualifies for Chapter 33 benefits.
bullets: Refer to the current VA guidance.
Verify if the student has a 10-year service obligation.

slides.headline: Examine the 85/15 rule
slides.body: Ensure the student meets the 85/15 enrollment threshold.
bullets: Refer to the relevant VA guidance.
Check if the student is taking at least 85% of the credit hours needed.

slides.headline: Address any issues early
slides.body: Promptly address any discrepancies to avoid paperwork.
bullets: Refer to the SAA letter for guidance.
Communicate with the student about next steps.

outline.caption: Learn to streamline intake with these five steps to ensure compliance and avoid paperwork.

rubricBreakdown.critique: The weakest slide is Step 5, which could benefit from a more concise headline to maintain the flow and readability of the carousel.
